name: Genomics England
description: Genomics England hosts and provides access to whole genome sequencing data from the 100,000 Genomes Project, covering approximately 85,000 NHS patients with rare diseases and cancer. Provides a secure research environment with linked clinical and genomic data to enable genomic medicine research.
population_coverage: NHS patients with rare diseases and cancer (specific cohorts)
tre_platform: Genomics England Research Environment (GERE) - Linux Virtual Desktop with HPC cluster (Helix)

requirements.name: GE DAC approval
requirements.description: Application reviewed by the Genomics England Data Access Committee for scientific merit and governance compliance

requirements.name: Researcher registration
requirements.description: All researchers must be registered and approved before accessing the research environment

requirements.name: GERE access agreement
requirements.description: Sign the Genomics England data access agreement governing terms of use within the research environment

access_steps.name: Submit research application
access_steps.description: Apply to the Genomics England Data Access Committee with research proposal, data requirements, and team details

access_steps.name: DAC review
access_steps.description: Data Access Committee reviews application for scientific merit, governance, and alignment with participant consent

access_steps.name: Researcher onboarding
access_steps.description: Approved researchers complete training, agreements, and technical onboarding for the GERE platform

timeline.notes: Preparing research application and gathering supporting documentation
DAC review process - can be lengthy for complex applications
Researcher onboarding, training, and GERE workspace setup

costs.tre_fee: Free for academic; industry via partnership fees
costs.notes: Academic research is free. Industry access available through Genomics England partnership agreements with associated fees.

strengths: Unique whole genome sequencing data linked to clinical phenotypes
Deep clinical linkage for rare disease and cancer cohorts
Focused rare disease and cancer genomics - unmatched depth in these areas
High-performance computing (Helix HPC) for genomic analysis
Growing data resource with Newborn Genomes Programme
limitations: Specific populations only (rare disease and cancer patients) - not population-representative
Requires genomics/bioinformatics expertise to analyse data
No data extraction - all work within GERE
recent_changes: Newborn Genomes Programme launched for genomic screening
Expanded cancer and rare disease cohorts
Improved GERE platform with enhanced HPC capabilities
